title: Fractures
summary: A fracture is a broken or cracked bone. Improper handling can worsen the injury or cause nerve and blood vessel damage.
steps: Tell the person to stay still. Do not move them unless in immediate danger.
Immobilize the injured area using a splint (stiff material like a board) padded with soft material.
Tie the splint in place above and below the fracture — not directly on it.
If bleeding, apply gentle pressure around (not over) the wound.
Elevate the injury if possible to reduce swelling.
Apply ice or a cold pack wrapped in cloth to reduce swelling.
Monitor circulation below the fracture — check for numbness, pallor, or coldness.
what_to_do: Keep the injured limb still and supported
Splint the bone in the position found
Apply cold pack to reduce swelling
Elevate the injured area if safe to do so
what_not_to_do: Do NOT try to straighten or realign the bone
Do NOT move the person if you suspect a spine or neck fracture
Do NOT tie a splint too tightly — it can cut off circulation
Do NOT allow the person to walk on a fractured limb
Do NOT give food or water (surgery may be needed)
when_to_call_ambulance: Suspected fracture of the spine, neck, or skull
Bone is visible through the skin (open fracture)
Severe swelling, deformity, or inability to move the limb
Signs of blood vessel or nerve damage (numbness, tingling, loss of color)
